title: Angkor Wat Stories in the Bas-Reliefs: The Churning of the Ocean of ...
highlights: Hidden Stories in the Bas-Reliefs: The Churning of the Ocean of Milk and Depictions of Devis Angkor Wat Stories, One of the most fascinating and symbolic stories depicted in the bas-reliefs of Angkor Wat is the Churning of the Ocean of Milk. This story, drawn from Hindu mythology, is a cosmic tale of the gods (Devas) and demons (Asuras) who, in a fierce battle for immortality, churn the ocean to extract the amrita—the elixir of life. In the Angkor Wat bas-reliefs, this scene comes to life with great detail. The gods and demons are shown pulling a massive serpent, Vasuki, wrapped around Mount Mandara, using it as a rope to churn the ocean. As the churning  ...
